title: Frank Laezza | VP Finance and Operations (VPFO)

llm_payload.name: Frank Laezza
llm_payload.description: Frank Laezza is the Vice-President, Finance & Operations (VPFO) at the University of British Columbia, where he leads a team of over 1400 staff across key operational areas including finance, facilities, safety, risk and assurance on both the Vancouver and Okanagan campuses. Reporting directly to the President and Vice-Chancellor, Frank is a member of UBC’s executive leadership team, representing the VPFO portfolio in collaboration with internal and external partners to support and advance the university’s academic mission through the stewardship and continuous improvement of its physical and financial assets.
llm_payload.address: Koerner Library 6th Floor, 1958 Main Mall, Vancouver, BC, Canada V6T 1Z2

label: highly_relevant
title: Meet the Provost | UBC Provost & VP Academic
source_query: UBC Office of the Vice President contact

llm_payload.name: Dr. Gage Averill
llm_payload.description: Dr. Gage Averill is the Provost and Vice-President, Academic, UBC Vancouver. He provides vision and leadership to the Faculties, oversees the budget, leads institutional planning, and ensures UBC maintains excellence in its delivery of undergraduate, graduate and professional education. He previously served as Dean in the Faculty of Arts and held positions at the University of Toronto and New York University.

title: UBC Health Governance | UBC Health
source_query: UBC Office of the Vice President contact

llm_payload.name: Office of the Vice-President, Health
llm_payload.description: UBC Health works under the Office of the Vice-President, Health to enhance and enable interprofessional and collaborative health education and research to train people, develop knowledge, and shape policy—seeking to address inequities and improve the systems that produce health. The Office of the Vice-President, Health advances these aims by promoting and facilitating equitable collaborations across disciplines and faculties at UBC’s Vancouver and Okanagan campuses that collectively contribute to UBC Health, as well as with communities, institutions, and government organizations around the province.
